nodes.label: Biopsy-proven solitary lesion of bone or soft tissue with clonal plasma cells, normal skeletal survey/MRI of spine/pelvis, absence of myeloma-defining events, and clonal BMPCs <10%
Radiation therapy (Primary treatment) - Preferred: RT to involved site: 40-50 Gy in 1.8-2.0 Gy fractions (20-25 fractions). For plasmacytomas <5 cm, 35-40 Gy is acceptable [MYEL-2, MYEL-D].
Surgery (If structurally unstable or neurologic compromise) - Useful in certain circumstances: Consider surgery for instability or mass effect [MYEL-2].
Clinical trial (Alternative option) - Useful in certain circumstances: Consider clinical trial participation [MYEL-2].
Low risk (0 risk factors per 20/2/20: BMPCs >20%, M-protein >2 g/dL, FLCr >20)
High risk (≥2 risk factors per 20/2/20 criteria)
Observation (Active monitoring) - Category 1: Observe at 3-6 month intervals with regular lab assessments and imaging [MYEL-3].
Clinical trial (Alternative) - Useful in certain circumstances: Enroll in clinical trial if available [MYEL-3].
Clinical trial (Preferred option) - Preferred: Strongly recommend enrollment in ongoing clinical trial [MYEL-3].
Systemic therapy (For select patients) - Useful in certain circumstances: Consider single-agent daratumumab (category 1) or lenalidomide (category 2B) based on shared decision-making [MYEL-3].
Observation (Close monitoring) - Category 1: Observe at 3-month intervals as clinically indicated, with prompt initiation of therapy upon progression [MYEL-3].

flowcharts.title: Management of Newly Diagnosed Symptomatic Multiple Myeloma

nodes.label: HCT eligibility, frailty status, cytogenetic risk, patient preferences
HCT-eligible patients (all ages, no absolute contraindications)
HCT-ineligible or deferred (age >70, significant comorbidities, frailty, or patient choice)

Primary therapy (induction) - Quadruplet regimen preferred: Dara-VRd (daratumumab/bortezomib/lenalidomide/dexamethasone) or Isa-VRd (isatuximab-irfc/bortezomib/lenalidomide/dexamethasone) [category 1]. Collect stem cells within first 6 cycles [MYEL-4, MYEL-G].

Autologous HCT (Post-induction consolidation) - Category 1: Proceed to high-dose melphalan and autologous HCT after induction. Collect sufficient stem cells for up to 2 transplants if appropriate [MYEL-5].

Maintenance therapy (Post-HCT) - Category 1: Lenalidomide maintenance [category 1]. For high-risk MM, consider doublet maintenance (e.g., carfilzomib/lenalidomide, daratumumab/lenalidomide) [MYEL-5, MYEL-G].
Primary therapy (Quadruplet or triplet regimen) - Preferred: Dara-Rd (daratum
